content.text:
## Task: Fix [medium] Documentation: Verification checklist still describes inline Download/Delete/Move/Copy buttons, not the new ⋮ dropd
## Step: execute

### README.md
```markdown
# File Browser

A full-stack file browser with a .NET Web API backend and a TypeScript SPA frontend.

## Prerequisites

- [.NET 10 SDK](https://dotnet.microsoft.com/download/dotnet/10.0)
- [Node.js v20+](https://nodejs.org/)
- npm (ships with Node.js)

## Quick Start

Run the following commands in order from the repository root.

```bash
# 1. Install JavaScript dependencies
npm install

# 2. Build the frontend
#    Compiles TypeScript in src/ to wwwroot/dist/ and copies src/app.css to wwwroot/dist/app.css
npm run build

# 3. Start the backend server
dotnet run --project TestProject.csproj
```

Open the HTTPS URL printed in the console (e.g. `https://localhost:7146`). Click **Browse Files** to open the file-browser dialog.

## Configuration

The server's home directory (where files are stored) is configurable via the `FileService:HomeDirectory` setting.

- **appsettings.json**: set `FileService.HomeDirectory` (default is `"Home"`).
- **Environment variable**: set `FileService__HomeDirectory` (double underscore).

Relative paths resolve under the app content root. The folder is created automatically on first use (lazily, the first time a file-system operation targets it), so a misconfigured path surfaces on the first request rather than at startup.

## Limits

- **Search results are capped at 500.** Search stops collecting matches once it reaches 500 results, so a broad query may return fewer entries than actually exist. Narrow the query or scope it to a subfolder to see additional matches.
- **Directory copy is limited to 32 levels of nesting.** Copying a tree nested more than 32 levels deep aborts with an IOException whose message is `Directory copy depth limit exceeded (possible cycle)`, to guard against stack overflow on pathological or self-referential directory trees.

## File name handling

Uploaded file names are sanitized before they touch disk, and the rules are enforced identically on every platform so an upload that works on Linux is also valid on Windows:

- **Directory components are stripped.** A submitted name like `../x` or `..\x` is reduced to its final segment (`x`), so an upload can never traverse out of its target directory. Both `/` and `\` are treated as separators.
- **Windows-incompatible names are rejected** with a `400 Bad Request`: names containing characters invalid on Windows (`< > : " | ? * \ /`) or matching a reserved device name (`CON`, `PRN`, `AUX`, `NUL`, `COM1`–`COM9`, `LPT1`–`LPT9`, with or without an extension).

## Symlinks and junctions

Search and copy never follow symbolic links (Linux/macOS) or directory junctions (Windows). A link is still listed by name in browse and search results, but its contents are never scanned or copied, so a link that targets a location outside the home directory cannot leak external files into a search or copy.

## Deep Linking

The app keeps its current view and location in the URL hash, so any folder listing or search result can be bookmarked or shared as a direct link.

- `#/browse` — root folder listing.
- `#/browse/Documents/Reports` — a nested folder.
- `#/search?q=budget&path=Documents` — a search scoped to a folder.

Each path segment is percent-encoded (e.g. spaces become `%20`).

## Running Tests

```bash
# Frontend tests (Vitest)
npm test

# Backend tests (xUnit)
dotnet test TestProject.Tests/TestProject.Tests.csproj
```

## Final End-to-End Verification Checklist

1. **`dotnet build TestProject.csproj`** — project compiles without errors.
2. **`npm test`** — all frontend tests pass.
3. **`npm run build`** — succeeds and produces `wwwroot/dist/app.js` and `wwwroot/dist/app.css`.
4. **`dotnet run`** — serves `index.html` at `/` (visit `https://localhost:7146`).
5. **`GET /api/files/browse?path=`** — returns JSON with `entries`, `folderCount`, `fileCount`, and `totalSize`.
6. **UI dialog** — opens when "Browse Files" is clicked.
7. **UI lists files/folders** — entries render in the table with Name, Size, Modified, and Actions columns.
8. **Upload** — file upload works via the Upload button.
9. **Download** — clicking the Download link retrieves the file.
10. **Delete** — Delete action removes files/folders after confirmation.
11. **Move** — Move action renames/relocates entries via a prompt.
12. **Copy** — Copy action duplicates entries via a prompt.
13. **Search** — Search input and button filter entries recursively by name.

```
You are a fix agent. Resolve the following final-review finding.

Review dimension: Documentation
Severity: medium
File: README.md:82-87
Title: Verification checklist still describes inline Download/Delete/Move/Copy buttons, not the new ⋮ dropdown / right-click menu

Finding:
The "Final End-to-End Verification Checklist" in README.md was written for the old UI where each browse row showed inline Download/Delete/Move/Copy controls. After this changeset those controls live behind a per-row "⋮" dropdown button (`.row-menu-btn`, revealed on row hover) and are ALSO available by right-clicking anywhere on a data row (which opens the same `.row-menu` at the cursor). The Actions column header is now intentionally blank (an empty `<th>`). The checklist contradicts this: item 7 says "entries render in the table with Name, Size, Modified, and Actions columns" (the column exists but its header is blank and the controls are no longer inline); items 9–12 say "clicking the Download link" / "Delete action" / "Move action" / "Copy action" as if the controls are directly visible in the row. A reviewer following this checklist literally would look for a Download link / Delete button in the row, not find one, and could wrongly conclude the feature is broken. The new primary interaction (the ⋮ dropdown) and the new right-click context menu are not mentioned anywhere in the README.

Fix instructions (follow exactly; make targeted, minimal changes):
Edit README.md and update the "Final End-to-End Verification Checklist" items so they match the current UI. (1) Item 7: change `entries render in the table with Name, Size, Modified, and Actions columns.` to reflect that the table still has four columns (Name, Size, Modified, Actions) but the Actions column header is intentionally blank and each data row exposes its actions via a "⋮" button that appears on row hover. Suggested text: `7. **UI lists files/folders** — entries render in a four-column table (Name, Size, Modified, and an Actions column whose header is intentionally blank); hover a row to reveal its "⋮" actions button.` (2) Rewrite items 9–12 so each action is reached by opening the "⋮" menu, and mention the right-click alternative. Suggested text: `9. **Download** — open a file row's "⋮" menu (or right-click the row) and choose Download to retrieve the file.` / `10. **Delete** — open the "⋮" menu (or right-click) and choose Delete; it removes files/folders after a confirmation.` / `11. **Move** — open the "⋮" menu (or right-click) and choose Move; it renames/relocates the entry via a prompt.` / `12. **Copy** — open the "⋮" menu (or right-click) and choose Copy; it duplicates the entry via a prompt.` (3) Add a short note (as a new item 8.5, e.g. `8.5. **Row actions menu** — hovering a browse row reveals a "⋮" button; clicking it (or right-clicking anywhere on the row) opens a dropdown of Download/Delete/Move/Copy actions. Press Escape or click elsewhere to close it; only one menu is open at a time.`). Leave all other sections (Quick Start, Configuration, Limits, Deep Linking, Running Tests, etc.) unchanged as they are unaffected by this change.

```bash

npm install

# 2. Build the frontend

npm run build

# 3. Start the backend server
dotnet run --project TestProject.csproj

```bash
# Frontend tests (Vitest)

# Backend tests (xUnit)
dotnet test TestProject.Tests/TestProject.Tests.csproj
```

## Final End-to-End Verification Checklist

1. **`dotnet build TestProject.csproj`** — project compiles without errors.
2. **`npm test`** — all frontend tests pass.
3. **`npm run build`** — succeeds and produces `wwwroot/dist/app.js` and `wwwroot/dist/app.css`.
4. **`dotnet run`** — serves `index.html` at `/` (visit `https://localhost:7146`).
5. **`GET /api/files/browse?path=`** — returns JSON with `entries`, `folderCount`, `fileCount`, and `totalSize`.
6. **UI dialog** — opens when "Browse Files" is clicked.
7. **UI lists files/folders** — entries render in the table with Name, Size, Modified, and Actions columns.
8. **Upload** — file upload works via the Upload button.
9. **Download** — clicking the Download link retrieves the file.
10. **Delete** — Delete action removes files/folders after confirmation.
11. **Move** — Move action renames/relocates entries via a prompt.
12. **Copy** — Copy action duplicates entries via a prompt.
13. **Search** — Search input and button filter entries recursively by name.

content.thinking:
Now I'll make the targeted edits per the fix instructions. I need to:
1. Update item 7
2. Add a new item 8.5 (Row actions menu)
3. Rewrite items 9-12

Let me edit these as a single edit covering items 7-12 since they're contiguous.

content.text: Now I'll apply the targeted edits to the verification checklist items 7–12, adding the new item 8.5 as instructed.

edits.oldText:
7. **UI lists files/folders** — entries render in the table with Name, Size, Modified, and Actions columns.
8. **Upload** — file upload works via the Upload button.
9. **Download** — clicking the Download link retrieves the file.
10. **Delete** — Delete action removes files/folders after confirmation.
11. **Move** — Move action renames/relocates entries via a prompt.
12. **Copy** — Copy action duplicates entries via a prompt.
edits.newText:
7. **UI lists files/folders** — entries render in a four-column table (Name, Size, Modified, and an Actions column whose header is intentionally blank); hover a row to reveal its "⋮" actions button.
8. **Upload** — file upload works via the Upload button.
8.5. **Row actions menu** — hovering a browse row reveals a "⋮" button; clicking it (or right-clicking anywhere on the row) opens a dropdown of Download/Delete/Move/Copy actions. Press Escape or click elsewhere to close it; only one menu is open at a time.
9. **Download** — open a file row's "⋮" menu (or right-click the row) and choose Download to retrieve the file.
10. **Delete** — open the "⋮" menu (or right-click) and choose Delete; it removes files/folders after a confirmation.
11. **Move** — open the "⋮" menu (or right-click) and choose Move; it renames/relocates the entry via a prompt.
12. **Copy** — open the "⋮" menu (or right-click) and choose Copy; it duplicates the entry via a prompt.

content.text:
1. **`dotnet build TestProject.csproj`** — project compiles without errors.
2. **`npm test`** — all frontend tests pass.
3. **`npm run build`** — succeeds and produces `wwwroot/dist/app.js` and `wwwroot/dist/app.css`.
4. **`dotnet run`** — serves `index.html` at `/` (visit `https://localhost:7146`).
5. **`GET /api/files/browse?path=`** — returns JSON with `entries`, `folderCount`, `fileCount`, and `totalSize`.
6. **UI dialog** — opens when "Browse Files" is clicked.
7. **UI lists files/folders** — entries render in a four-column table (Name, Size, Modified, and an Actions column whose header is intentionally blank); hover a row to reveal its "⋮" actions button.
8. **Upload** — file upload works via the Upload button.
8.5. **Row actions menu** — hovering a browse row reveals a "⋮" button; clicking it (or right-clicking anywhere on the row) opens a dropdown of Download/Delete/Move/Copy actions. Press Escape or click elsewhere to close it; only one menu is open at a time.
9. **Download** — open a file row's "⋮" menu (or right-click the row) and choose Download to retrieve the file.
10. **Delete** — open the "⋮" menu (or right-click) and choose Delete; it removes files/folders after a confirmation.
11. **Move** — open the "⋮" menu (or right-click) and choose Move; it renames/relocates the entry via a prompt.
12. **Copy** — open the "⋮" menu (or right-click) and choose Copy; it duplicates the entry via a prompt.
13. **Search** — Search input and button filter entries recursively by name.

[1 more lines in file. Use offset=90 to continue.]

The edits have been applied successfully. This is a documentation-only change (no code modifications), so no compilation or tests are required.

The changes match the fix instructions exactly:
1. **Item 7** updated to reflect the four-column table with a blank Actions column header and the hover-revealed "⋮" button.
2. **New item 8.5** added describing the row actions menu (⋮ button, right-click alternative, Escape/click-to-close, single-menu constraint).
3. **Items 9–12** rewritten so each action (Download/Delete/Move/Copy) is reached by opening the "⋮" menu, with the right-click alternative noted.

All other sections (Quick Start, Configuration, Limits, File name handling, Symlinks, Deep Linking, Running Tests, and checklist items 1–6, 8, 13) remain unchanged as they are unaffected by this change.
